original_abstract:
This paper introduces **Top++ (v2.0)**, a C++ program designed for the high-precision numerical calculation of the inclusive top-quark pair production cross-section at hadron colliders. The software incorporates the most advanced theoretical developments in Quantum Chromodynamics (QCD), offering exact fixed-order results up to next-to-next-to-leading order (NNLO) and threshold resummation in Mellin space at next-to-next-to-leading logarithmic (NNLL) accuracy. 

A primary focus of **Top++** is accessibility and flexibility. The program features a comprehensive user interface with 29 configurable options, allowing for extensive studies of scale variations, top-quark mass dependence, and individual partonic channels. Version 2.0 introduces a robust framework for estimating PDF uncertainties, including pre-built prescriptions for major PDF sets such as MSTW, NNPDF, CT, and ABM. Engineered for performance, the code utilizes a second-order finite difference scheme for parton luminosity integration to ensure per-mill numerical accuracy without the overhead of Monte Carlo sampling. **Top++** is an open-source tool intended for both experimentalists and theorists, requiring no prior programming expertise for standard phenomenological applications.
humanized_abstract:
We present Top++ 2.0, a C++ code for high precision calculations of the inclusive top-quark pair production cross section at hadron colliders. Top++ 2.0 embodies the latest advances in quantum chromodynamics theory. It produces exact, fixed-order results to next-to-next-to-leading order (NNLO) of the strong interactions, together with the next-to-next-to-leading logarithmic threshold resummed to all orders in Mellin space.

Top++ focus is on ease of use and flexibility. The program includes a flexible user interface with 29 parameters to allow a large number of mass scans, studies of the impact of the top quark mass and analyses of the impact of the different partonic channels. In version 2.0, this release we also have an improved way of treating PDF uncertainties, including a flexible framework, with several presets for all major PDF sets (MSTW, NNPDF, CT, ABM). For production of parton luminosity we use a second order finite difference scheme to obtain numerical precision of a mill or better without the need for the additional uncertainty of a Monte Carlo. The Top++ program is fully open source, allowing experiments and theorists to easily adapt it to their needs, without needing to know or learn a programming language.

document.input:
This paper introduces **Top++ (v2.0)**, a C++ program designed for the high-precision numerical calculation of the inclusive top-quark pair production cross-section at hadron colliders. The software incorporates the most advanced theoretical developments in Quantum Chromodynamics (QCD), offering exact fixed-order results up to next-to-next-to-leading order (NNLO) and threshold resummation in Mellin space at next-to-next-to-leading logarithmic (NNLL) accuracy. 

A primary focus of **Top++** is accessibility and flexibility. The program features a comprehensive user interface with 29 configurable options, allowing for extensive studies of scale variations, top-quark mass dependence, and individual partonic channels. Version 2.0 introduces a robust framework for estimating PDF uncertainties, including pre-built prescriptions for major PDF sets such as MSTW, NNPDF, CT, and ABM.
